title: [InstCombine] Preserve the flag from RHS only if the `and` is bitwise
summary: The generalization extends an existing FMF propagation optimization to handle logical AND (`select` with `false`) alongside bitwise AND. The key insight is that for logical AND, the right-hand side (RHS) guards execution of the left-hand side (LHS), so only the LHS's FMF should propagate—union with RHS FMF could incorrectly introduce poison. The patch adds a conditional check (`!IsLogicalSelect`) to avoid union with RHS FMF in guarded contexts, preserving correctness while allowing the same fold for both `and` and `select` patterns. This approach is reusable: when generalizing optimizations to logical operators, carefully consider which operands' flags propagate based on guarding semantics.
